module1.clean_text:
Re-hospitalization form:
This is the child [PATIENT] born on [DATE] currently aged [AGE] years and 7 [AGE] originally from and living in MOSTAGANEM, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed at the EPH BOUGUIRAT MOSTAGANEM since [DATE] for a primary immunodeficiency such as congenital agammaglobulinemia, hospitalized at our level today for the immunoglobulin treatment N: [AGE] Main diagnosis: Primary immunodeficiency type Congenital Agammaglobulinemia
Associated diagnoses: None
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] Age of diagnosis: 6 years and 4 [AGE] elements:
Anamnestics:
-A brother died in [DATE] in respiratory distress at the Canastel pediatric intensive care unit (IMC due to meningitis at the age of [AGE] A maternal uncle died at the age of [AGE] from unknown etiologies. Clinic:
-Recurrent purulent ear infections with arthritis of the left knee since the age of [AGE] -Chronic wet cough since the age of [AGE] Paraclinic:
-Immune deficiency assessment: done on: [DATE] 0.33 g/l IgA: 0.26 g/l IgM: 0.181 g/l. -Absence of B lymphocytes. The picture is in favor of congenital Agammaglobulinemia. NB: need to repeat the immune assessment for the child and for the parents (mother). Processing in progress:
-Itraconazole 1 tab/day
-Bactrim 1 tab day a day
-Physiotherapy
Repercussions of the illness:
Nutritionally:
Weight: 28,500 kg (- 0.5 DS)., Height: 134 cm (-0.3 DS) BMI: 15.9 (-0.7 DS)
GOMEZ: 89% Waterloo: 97% Slight malnutrition
On the respiratory level:
-6 min walking test: done without abnormalities. To be completed with spirometry
-Front chest x-ray: bilateral infiltrates in both pulmonary fields predominating in bilateral para hilar. -ECB and sputum parasitology: culture: polymorphic flora of commensal type. -Physiotherapy with therapeutic education. -Thoracic CT: 09/15/2025: bilateral parenchymal condensation involving the middle lobe and heterogeneous lingula by the presence of air bronchogram: aspect of multifocal bronchopneumopathy. On the cardiac level:
-Echocoeur: 09/17/2025: without anomalies. On the ENT level:
-An ENT opinion made for recurrent ear infections: objectivizing:
Bilateral tympanic retraction
Intermediate history: Since the last treatment: 02/22/2026
Appetite preserved. -Consultation to a private doctor for the appearance of a cough with greenish sputum, patient put on Binozyt 250 mg for 6 days. - physiotherapy done
-ECBC (12/28/2025): No germ
-Current treatment: Bactrim: 20 mg/kg/day /Itraconazole: 5 mg/kg/day. - Notebooks: well kept. -Dermato consultation not done, Immunodeficiency assessment not done. Biological assessment
-Residual IgG dosage: (02/16/2026) 13.5 g/dl
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: 03/25/2026
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 28,500 kg (- 0.5 DS)., Height: 134 cm (-0.3 DS) BMI: 15.9 (-0.7 DS)
GOMEZ: 89% Waterloo: 97% Slight malnutrition
General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 36.7 C, good mucocutaneous coloring. Good state of hydration. Examination of the skin and appendages:
No scalp lesions. Presence of scars from traumatic injuries on the left knee. Presence of small brownish hyperpigmentation lesions on the face. Head and neck examination:
No particular features, no craniofacial dysmorphia. Normally implanted ears. No cervical ADP. ENT examination: Absence of otorrhea, absence of ear pain. Clean throat, tooth decay
ENT opinion made: Bilateral tympanic retraction
Ophthalmic examination: Conjunctival hyperemia
Pleuropulmonary examination: Wet cough, whitish sputum
No chest deformation, no sign of chronic respiratory failure. FR: 22 cycles/min, SPO2: 99% under ambient air,
Pulmonary auscultation: polymorphic rales of bronchial congestion in both pulmonary fields. Cardiovascular examination: HR. : 80 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 clearly audible, no murmurs or added noises. The rest of the exam: Is unremarkable. HDJ support; Replacement immunotherapy at a rate of 0.7g/kg or 20g, brand: Intratect passed without incident
Ophthalmic opinion: Bilateral allergic conjunctivitis
ENT opinion made: Bilateral tympanic retraction
DISCHARGE Discharged the same day, after the end of the treatment.
Prophylactic treatment Bactrim 20mg/kg/d
Antifungal prophylaxis itraconazole orally 5mg/kg/day
Hygiene and diet advice
ETP and monitoring notebooks
Plan courses of multivalent immunoglobulin infusions every month. Control appointment at the CS DIP of Professor Drali
Reports requested:
Residual IgG dosage every 3 months
Review ENT control
Plan hearing assessment
Provide spirometry
Dermatological opinion
Stomatology advice
Recheck chest CT
Recover the assessment of the mother's immune deficiency
Predict aspergillus serology
CONCLUSION:
The child AZZEMMOU MAHDI aged 9 years and 7 months, originally from and residing in Mostaganem, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed for a DIP type agammaglobulinemia, Admitted to our level for his treatment of immunoglobulin No. 43. History of hospitalizations:

phenopacket.input:
Re-hospitalization form:
This is the child AZZEMMOU MAHDI born on 04/23/2016, currently aged 9 years and 7 months, originally from and living in MOSTAGANEM, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed at the EPH BOUGUIRAT MOSTAGANEM since 2022 for a primary immunodeficiency such as congenital agammaglobulinemia, hospitalized at our level today for the immunoglobulin treatment N: 43. Main diagnosis: Primary immunodeficiency type Congenital Agammaglobulinemia
Associated diagnoses: None
Date of diagnosis: 08/23/2022. Age of diagnosis: 6 years and 4 months
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics:
-A brother died in 2022 in respiratory distress at the Canastel pediatric intensive care unit (IMC due to meningitis at the age of 6)
- A maternal uncle died at the age of 18 months from unknown etiologies. Clinic:
-Recurrent purulent ear infections with arthritis of the left knee since the age of 5. -Chronic wet cough since the age of 5. Paraclinic:
-Immune deficiency assessment: done on: 08/23/2022:
-IgG: 0.33 g/l IgA: 0.26 g/l IgM: 0.181 g/l. -Absence of B lymphocytes. The picture is in favor of congenital Agammaglobulinemia. NB: need to repeat the immune assessment for the child and for the parents (mother). Processing in progress:
-Itraconazole 1 tab/day
-Bactrim 1 tab day a day
-Physiotherapy
Repercussions of the illness:
Nutritionally:
Weight: 28,500 kg (- 0.5 DS)., Height: 134 cm (-0.3 DS) BMI: 15.9 (-0.7 DS)
GOMEZ: 89% Waterloo: 97% Slight malnutrition
On the respiratory level:
-6 min walking test: done without abnormalities. To be completed with spirometry
-Front chest x-ray: bilateral infiltrates in both pulmonary fields predominating in bilateral para hilar. -ECB and sputum parasitology: culture: polymorphic flora of commensal type. -Physiotherapy with therapeutic education. -Thoracic CT: 09/15/2025: bilateral parenchymal condensation involving the middle lobe and heterogeneous lingula by the presence of air bronchogram: aspect of multifocal bronchopneumopathy. On the cardiac level:
-Echocoeur: 09/17/2025: without anomalies. On the ENT level:
-An ENT opinion made for recurrent ear infections: objectivizing:
Bilateral tympanic retraction
Intermediate history: Since the last treatment: 02/22/2026
Appetite preserved. -Consultation to a private doctor for the appearance of a cough with greenish sputum, patient put on Binozyt 250 mg for 6 days. - physiotherapy done
-ECBC (12/28/2025): No germ
-Current treatment: Bactrim: 20 mg/kg/day /Itraconazole: 5 mg/kg/day. - Notebooks: well kept. -Dermato consultation not done, Immunodeficiency assessment not done. Biological assessment
-Residual IgG dosage: (02/16/2026) 13.5 g/dl
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: 03/25/2026
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 28,500 kg (- 0.5 DS)., Height: 134 cm (-0.3 DS) BMI: 15.9 (-0.7 DS)
GOMEZ: 89% Waterloo: 97% Slight malnutrition
General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 36.7 C, good mucocutaneous coloring. Good state of hydration. Examination of the skin and appendages:
No scalp lesions. Presence of scars from traumatic injuries on the left knee. Presence of small brownish hyperpigmentation lesions on the face. Head and neck examination:
No particular features, no craniofacial dysmorphia. Normally implanted ears. No cervical ADP. ENT examination: Absence of otorrhea, absence of ear pain. Clean throat, tooth decay
ENT opinion made: Bilateral tympanic retraction
Ophthalmic examination: Conjunctival hyperemia
Pleuropulmonary examination: Wet cough, whitish sputum
No chest deformation, no sign of chronic respiratory failure. FR: 22 cycles/min, SPO2: 99% under ambient air,
Pulmonary auscultation: polymorphic rales of bronchial congestion in both pulmonary fields. Cardiovascular examination: HR. : 80 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 clearly audible, no murmurs or added noises. The rest of the exam: Is unremarkable. HDJ support; Replacement immunotherapy at a rate of 0.7g/kg or 20g, brand: Intratect passed without incident
Ophthalmic opinion: Bilateral allergic conjunctivitis
ENT opinion made: Bilateral tympanic retraction
DISCHARGE Discharged the same day, after the end of the treatment.
Prophylactic treatment Bactrim 20mg/kg/d
Antifungal prophylaxis itraconazole orally 5mg/kg/day
Hygiene and diet advice
ETP and monitoring notebooks
Plan courses of multivalent immunoglobulin infusions every month. Control appointment at the CS DIP of Professor Drali
Reports requested:
Residual IgG dosage every 3 months
Review ENT control
Plan hearing assessment
Provide spirometry
Dermatological opinion
Stomatology advice
Recheck chest CT
Recover the assessment of the mother's immune deficiency
Predict aspergillus serology
CONCLUSION:
The child AZZEMMOU MAHDI aged 9 years and 7 months, originally from and residing in Mostaganem, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed for a DIP type agammaglobulinemia, Admitted to our level for his treatment of immunoglobulin No. 43. History of hospitalizations:
